title: Multiple damage instances
principles: Multiple-instance delivery describes how many potential damage instances are produced by one attack event and must remain separate from attack sequences initiated by one input activation.
Instance count, contact independence, critical eligibility, and status eligibility are distinct properties and must not be inferred from one another.
Pellets, multishot-generated instances, repeated internal events, and separate damage components must remain distinguishable by their explicit source mechanism.
A supplied instance count establishes potential multiplicity but does not establish simultaneous timing, valid contact, independent critical rolls, independent status rolls, or multi-target reach.
Separate damage components count as separate instances only when the source establishes that each component can produce an independent damage event.
Base, conditional, and externally modified instance counts must remain separate.
Use neutral terms such as instance or damage event when the source does not explicitly identify the mechanism as pellets, projectiles, beams, or another defined form.

single_instance: The supplied data explicitly identify one potential damage instance for the evaluated attack event.
This classification must not be assigned solely from the absence of multi-instance evidence.
multiple_instances: The attack event produces more than one potential damage instance through a supplied and traceable mechanism.
Their practical contribution depends on contact independence and per-instance eligibility.
independent_contact_instances: The supplied mechanic establishes that multiple instances can produce separate valid contacts.
Independent contact does not automatically establish independent critical or status eligibility.
multi_component_events: The attack event contains several supplied damage components.
Count them as independent instances only when each component can produce a separate damage event.
conditional_multiplicity: The number of instances changes under a supplied condition, state, or mechanic.
Preserve the base count, modified count, and activation condition separately.
undetermined: The supplied fields do not establish the number, source, or independence of damage instances with sufficient confidence.

pellet_count_present: Treat pellet_count as explicit pellet multiplicity only when the source defines it for the evaluated attack event.
Do not assume identical spread, contact, critical eligibility, or status eligibility across all pellets.
multishot_present: Use multishot as instance-count evidence only when its effect on the evaluated mode is explicitly defined or reliably normalized.
Do not assume that multishot always produces visible projectiles, independent contacts, or additional ammunition consumption.
fire_iterations_source_field: When fire_iterations is the source representation of base multishot, normalize it as multishot rather than treating it as a separate instance mechanism.
Do not count both fire_iterations and multishot.
multiple_damage_components: Do not count listed components as separate instances unless their independent damage-event behavior is established.
direct_and_radial_components_present: Treat direct and radial components as independently eligible instances only when both can apply damage separately.
Do not assume shared critical chance, status chance, falloff, target eligibility, or contact behavior.

exceptions: Do not include burst count or other sequential attack events in the per-event instance count.
Do not assume that multishot consumes additional ammunition.
Do not infer independent critical or status rolls from instance count alone.
Do not add or multiply status chance linearly by the number of instances.
Do not infer multi-target capability from instance count alone.
Do not count the same source field twice after normalization.
